tool_definitions.name: lsp__golden-lsp__open_document
tool_definitions.description: Open a document in LSP server "golden-lsp" for analysis. Must be called before hover/definition/references.

tool_definitions.name: lsp__golden-lsp__close_document
tool_definitions.description: Close a previously opened document in LSP server "golden-lsp".

tool_definitions.name: lsp__golden-lsp__get_diagnostics
tool_definitions.description: Get cached diagnostics (compiler errors, warnings, lints) from LSP server "golden-lsp". Returns all diagnostics if no URI specified.

tool_definitions.name: lsp__golden-lsp__hover
tool_definitions.description: Get hover information (type, documentation) for a symbol at a position in LSP server "golden-lsp".

tool_definitions.name: lsp__golden-lsp__goto_definition
tool_definitions.description: Find the definition location of a symbol at a position in LSP server "golden-lsp".

tool_definitions.name: lsp__golden-lsp__find_references
tool_definitions.description: Find all references to a symbol at a position in LSP server "golden-lsp". Returns up to 100 results.

tool_definitions.name: lsp__golden-lsp__document_symbols
tool_definitions.description: List all symbols (functions, classes, variables) in a document in LSP server "golden-lsp". Returns up to 50 results.

tool_definitions.name: lsp__golden-lsp__workspace_symbols
tool_definitions.description: Search for symbols across the workspace in LSP server "golden-lsp". Returns up to 50 results.

steps.message: First use the "lsp__golden-lsp__open_document" tool to open a document with uri "file:///src/main.ts" and content "function greet(name: string) { return name; }". Then use the "lsp__golden-lsp__hover" tool to get hover info at uri "file:///src/main.ts", line 0, character 9. Report the hover result.

results.content:
Sure! I'll start by opening the document first, and then fetch the hover info once it's open. Let me do these sequentially.

**Step 1: Open the document**

Then use the "lsp__golden-lsp__hover" tool to get hover info at uri "file:///src/main.ts", line 0, character 9. Report the hover result.

results.content:
Here are the results from both steps:

### Step 1: Open Document ✅
The document at `file:///src/main.ts` was successfully opened with the provided content.

---

### Step 2: Hover Result 📋

The hover information returned for **line 0, character 9** (which corresponds to the `greet` function name) is:

- **Type Signature:**
  ```typescript
  function greet(name: string): string
  ```
- **Documentation:** `Says hello.`
- **Range:** The hover applies to characters 9–14 on line 0, which is exactly the span of the word `greet`.

In summary, the LSP server recognizes `greet` as a function that accepts a `name` parameter of type `string` and returns a `string`, with the documentation note "Says hello."
